body_html:
The beatles on George Martins arms were great
--- Alastair Bruce a.bofc@virgin.net wrote:

Hi there,

It is indeed possible. However, I do not want to
mess you around and I
probably need to check that you are someone to whom
the UK can Grant Arms.

Either way, if you are looking for a good design,
the first question is for
you to answer ... what aspects of your life would
you like alluded to? There
may be things like you profession, place of birth,
family, chief interests
etc that you like most. There may be colours that
are significant - perhaps
the College/uni you attended - the Law school -
Tennis team etc .... These
are all useful. There may be a motto you like,
animal you think has some
association with your life etc.

It may seem random but all these things really help.

Have been asked to Scotland shooting next week and
leaving tonight. You must
think me such a layabout. Will be back on Tues 10
Oct.

Alastair

-----Original Message-----
From: J. Epstein [mailto:jeeproject@yahoo.com]
Sent: 28 September 2006 03:43
To: Alastair Bruce
Subject: RE:

Is itpossible to start the design process, before
the
granting, I can research my family tree, but the
creative process can start asap

--- Alastair Bruce a.bofc@virgin.net wrote:

Dear Mr Epstein,

Yesterday I spoke with Garter King of Arms, Peter
Gwynn-Jones, about the
plan we have for Granting you Arms. I mentioned
your clear caveat that it
must remain confidential.

Garter is very keen to help you with the Grant,
assuming it falls within his
remit to do so, and feels sure that although the
Grant itself is written "To
all and singular", which you will know is the old
fashioned way of making a
document address everyone, there is no casual
ability for the general public
to access the records at the College of Arms - and
none do.

In order to Grant Arms we will need to establish
if
a forebear of yours was
a British subject, or domiciled in the United
Kingdom. If so, as a
descendant, you fall under our remit. If not, we
may have to think again
about how we could do this.

I hope these details will not put you off and, if
it
amuses you, please look
at the College of Arms website.

www.college-of-arms.gov.uk

It would be a great pleasure for me to help you
and
I think you will enjoy
the process of selecting and designing your own
personal version of the
oldest and most colourful system of personal
identity in western culture.

With very best wishes,

Alastair

-----Original Message-----
From: J. Epstein [mailto:jeeproject@yahoo.com]
Sent: 16 August 2006 07:30
To: a.bofc@virgin.net
Subject:

alistair,, what about starting the process. I
think
it
would be fun,, but must remain extremely
confidential,, if this is not possible,, let's
not
